author: @RustyValveMusic
text:
Hi Rob, there aren't many people on Youtube who deal with batteries and relevant patents like you do.  Have you tried looking into a video here on Youtube called "Making high-performance batteries from junkyard scraps"?  It seems to be a simple and easy way to make batteries at home from what i can understand in the white paper. Any chance you could do a video on it?

Cheers

author: @Buzzhumma
text: You can actually buy them unassembled without the 18650 cells

author: @Buzzhumma
text: Hey rob and teem .i been making a few batteries of late and got to thinking of a simple way to use it in an everyday useful way and charging a phone is something we all do so I remembered a solar battery bank I bought off eBay 3 years ago . I felt it would be a wonderful affordable project idea for your work shop sometime that people could work towards . Check out the product here . You can take out the 18650 cells and replace it with cell that are made in your work shop ! Hope you feel it’s of help and a good idea 💡. Regards Andy https://rover.ebay.com/rover/0/0/0?mpre=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ebay.com.au%2Fulk%2Fitm%2F232073949363

replies.author: @albertmueller6616

I for one am extremely grateful. My father was a chemist and always wished for me to fallow him. Sadly I never had an interest until after he was gone . Now suddenly I realize just how much knowledge and hard work I lost the benefit of so easily getting so I am very grateful for all you do for us. Cheers Mate

author: @chrissweet2309
text: Hi Robert, I am super excited to join you on this build the battery webinar, thank you for the opportunity to learn a little bit from you. If I could I would definitely come out and visit you at your lab in the UK. Alas I cannot so this seems to be a great way to learn more. I have a question however I plan on trying to use this type of battery architecture to make a battery that would act as an external battery for my cell phone I am wondering how much active material we are getting in the kit and I'm assuming it's not enough,  about how much more active material would I need to make a 2200 milliamp hour battery pack that's all carbon I would love to do that if I could. Thanks for your time, I cannot wait for the webinar I'm so excited

author: @DanielSMatthews
text: Hi Rob, why do you think that people have a go at me when I point out that lightweight batteries are best used in mobile applications and particularly if they are of a type that contains rare materials that it makes more sense to use other technologies in fixed applications? e.g.. The stuff Telsla sell. Ignorance, commercial self interest, fanboyism...?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I have no idea mate

replies.author: @DanielSMatthews
replies.text: It seems nuts but some people do, to me technology is just an engineering recipe, you use the one that gets the results you want for a particular situation.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: indeed mate

replies.author: @jameswoll
replies.text: Make sure you click the little bell icon next to the "Subscribe" button for notifications. I just use the subscriptions page. Hope this helped!

author: @stemcampprojects7951
text:
OUTSTANDING - I'm really looking forward to this.  New sound cable is working well.  I like the thought of having a couple of friends build a kit at the same time - local feedback and pacing will help all of us.  This may be the wrong place to ask - but just for fun -- if you were designing this workshop with two grafoil electrodes and matching carbon-based elements using pva binder, what material would you use for the carbon?  Thanks again for all the information you've shared!  Oh, and thank you very much for making the time of the seminar friendly to folks like me in California!
Cheers

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome mate - and having a couple of guys work through it was for exactly the reasons you point out - it slows the pace - gives everyone else time to do the work and find their own questions and gives time for ideas to percolate - so it'll be a few hours long as an event not a couple of minutes - as for the carbon - there is no easy answer - what i would do is exeriment to find a new carbon and use that - i have done quite  few things on making various carbons so i would give them a go - cheers
